title: caucalis infesta subsp. neglecta
intro: Caucalis infesta subsp. neglecta, also known as wild carrot, is a biennial plant belonging to the Apiaceae family, native to Europe and parts of Asia. The medicinal properties of Caucalis infesta subsp. neglecta include its ability to act as a diuretic, reducing fluid retention and relieving urinary issues. Its health benefits include treating gout, kidney stones, and other urinary tract problems due to its high concentration of essential oils and flavonoids. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
